THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE WAS REPORTED EAST OF FORT PARKER SP ALONG LCRS 456 AND 406. AN UNOCCUPIED TRAILER WAS ROLLED OVER AND DESTROYED, METAL FENCES AND AWNINGS WERE DAMAGED, LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN, AND SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED IN SATURATED SOILS.

WIDESPREAD THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE POINT ENTERPRISE AREA; IN PARTICULAR ALONG LCRS 502, 504 AND 498. METAL SHEETING WAS TORN OFF BARNS, CARPORTS AND SHEDS, LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE KNOCKED DOWN OR SPLIT, POWER LINES WERE KNOCKED DOWN AND AT LEAST 2 VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED BY TREE LIMBS FALLING ON THEM.
LARGE TREE BLOWN OVER ONTO HOME AT FM 39 AND LCR 458. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG LCR 502 IN POINT ENTERPRISE. THIS SPIN-UP OCCURRED WITHIN A LARGER AREA OF STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE, BUT IT WAS SEEN BY AREA STORM SPOTTERS, AND ITS PRESENCE WAS EVIDENT IN PICTURES OF GRASS THAT HAD BEEN LAID DOWN IN A SWIRLING PATTERN WITHIN 2 FIELDS.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE ASSOCIATED TO AN EF1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WIND OF 110 MPH. THE MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE WAS FOUND TO SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREES, WITH A FEW OUTBUILDINGS DAMAGED OR DESTROYED NEAR THE COMMUNITY OF DIRECT. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED AS IT CROSSED THE PAT MAYSE LAKE.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 115 MPH. THIS TORNADO STARTED JUST TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST OF THE POWDERLY/CAMP MAXEY AREA. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DAMAGED/SNAPPED, AND A FEW HOMES SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED AS IT CROSSED INTO RED RIVER COUNTY.

*** 1 INJ *** A TORNADO BEGAN TO THE WEST OF IVANHOE ALONG CR 1120. MINOR HOUSE DAMAGE AND NOTABLE TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED. THE TORNADO DID ADDITIONAL DAMAGE TO HOMES, STRUCTURES AND TREES BETWEEN SH 78 AND FM 273. THE TORNADO INTENSIFIED AS IT APPROACHED FM 273 IN IVANHOE. TO THE EAST OF THE ROAD, A BRICK HOME LOST ITS ROOF, AND A DOUBLE WIDE WAS ROLLED AND DESTROYED. THIS IS WHERE THE ONE INJURY OCCURRED. FARTHER EAST OF THIS LOCATION, ALONG CR 2210, ANOTHER HOME SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO A LARGE METAL SHOP. FROM THIS POINT, THE TORNADO TRACKED IN MOSTLY OPEN LAND, BUT COULD BE TRACKED IN THE TREES BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST WEST OF CR 2275. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF-2 BECAUSE OF THE DAMAGE TO THE TREES AND HOMES IN THE IVANHOE AREA. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE AROUND 120 MPH.
